result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the project builds cleanly with the existing `NeedsQuoting` untouched.

- `EscapeField` returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it wraps it in double-quotes and doubles internal quotes.
- `JoinRow` escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- `SplitRow` scans the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is parsed until the un-doubled closing quote (doubled quotes become one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise it reads up to the next comma or end of line. The empty line yields one empty field, and a trailing comma yields a trailing empty field.

`dotnet build` passes with 0 warnings and 0 errors. I also ran a throwaway round-trip check from a temp directory (deleted afterward, nothing added to the project): all cases pass, including empty fields, embedded commas/quotes/CRLF, quote-only fields, and the empty-list/empty-line special cases from the spec.
